title: geonoma pohliana subsp. gastoniana
intro: Geonoma pohliana subsp. gastoniana, also known as Gaston's geonoma, is a subspecies of palm tree native to the tropical forests of South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Gaston's geonoma include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Traditionally, the plant is prepared by drying and then making herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
